title: Kimi K3: Open Frontier Intelligence
author: vincent_s
relevanceReason: 命中 3 个 AI/科技关键词。
textRawHtml:
https://www.kimi.com/en

Kimi K3 Intelligence, Performance & Price Analysis: https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k3
textZhHtml:
https://www.kimi.com/en

Kimi K3 智能、性能与价格分析： https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k3
summaryRaw: Today, we are introducing Kimi K3 — our most capable model. Kimi K3 is a 2.8T-parameter model built on our Kimi Delta Attention and Attention Residuals, with native vision capabilities and a 1-million-token context window. It is the world's first open 3T-class model, designed for frontier intelligence across long-horizon coding, knowledge work, and reasoning. While its overall performance still trails the most powerful proprietary models, Claude Fable 5 and GPT 5.6 Sol, Kimi K3 demonstrated frontier-level performance across our evaluation suite, consistently outperforming other tested models.

Kimi K3 is available today on Kimi.com, Kimi Work, Kimi Code, and the Kimi API. At launch, Kimi K3 will use max thinking effort by default, with low- and high-effort modes to be introduced in subsequent updates. We are currently working closely with inference partners and open-source maintainers to align technical details and ensure a reliable rollout across the ecosystem. The full model weights will be released by July 27, 2026. Further details on the architecture, training, and evaluations will be released alongside the Kimi K3 technical report. An Open 3T-Class Model Kimi K3 is the first open model to reach 2.8 trillion parameters. It marks the latest step in Kimi's sustained push at the scaling frontier: for nine of the past twelve months, Kimi models have set the upper bound of open-model sizes.

Kimi K3 is built on Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) and Attention Residuals (AttnRes), two architectural updates designed to improve how information flows across sequence length and model depth. We have also scaled up Mixture of Experts (MoE) sparsity, effectively activating 16 out of 896 experts when paired with a Stable LatentMoE framework. Together with refined training and data recipes, these structural changes yield an approximate 2.5× improvement in overall scaling efficiency compared to Kimi K2, allowing the model to convert compute into intelligence more effectively. αwKDAαwStable LatentMoEαwGated MLAαwStable LatentMoEwα3×1×Block n−1Block n−2Block n−3EmbeddingRouterLinear12123NNormLinearShared ExpertRouted ExpertLinearConvL2LinearConvL2LinearConvσσLinearσKimi Delta AttentionNormLinearOutput Coding Kimi K3 has strong long-horizon coding performance. Operating with minimal human oversight, it can sustain long engineering sessions, navigate massive repositories, and orchestrate terminal tools. Kimi K3 also excels in tasks blending software engineering with visual reasoning — it leverages screenshots and visuals to optimize game dev, frontend, and CAD. The case studies below show how Kimi K3's coding capability translates into open-ended software creation and scientific research. Kernel Optimization We tested the models' capability to optimize GPU kernels. Each model works independently in an identical sandbox, with up to 24 hours to profile, rewrite, and benchmark four tasks spanning AttnRes, KDA, and a 512-head-dimension MLA kernel across NVIDIA H200 and GPGPU from an alternative vendor. Kimi K3 performed competitively with Fable 5 (with fallback) and substantially outperformed Opus 4.8, GPT 5.6 Sol, and GPT 5.5.

Claude Fable 5 was evaluated by a third party, and its results may include fallback behavior. Across most models, some trajectories include small, acceptable precision shortcuts that remain within our numerical tolerance. GPGPU denotes general-purpose GPUs used for computation beyond graphics rendering. In the late stages of Kimi K3 development, an early version of Kimi K3 handled the majority of the team's kernel optimization works. GPU Compiler Development We further tested whether Kimi K3 could build a GPU programming system from scratch. Kimi K3 developed MiniTriton, a compact Triton-like compiler with its own tile-level IR layer over MLIR, optimization passes, and a PTX code-generation pipeline. Across supported roofline benchmarks, MiniTriton delivers performance on par with or better than Triton and torch.compile — beating Triton on certain workloads. Beyond microbenchmarks, MiniTriton sustains end-to-end nanoGPT training with stable convergence, the loss curve closely tracking the reference with only minor divergence — validating the full pipeline on a realistic workload. These results demonstrate that Kimi K3 can build a coherent end-to-end compiler — from DSL frontend and IR passes to PTX codegen and runtime — rather than isolated kernels; its from-scratch Tensor Core path already rivals Triton’s extensively optimized stack.

Game Dev and Digital Creation Kimi K3 combines strong 3D reasoning, coding, and vision capabilities to turn concepts, images, and videos into fully playable interactive experiences. Kimi K3 achieves true "vision in the loop" by seamlessly iterating between code and live screenshots—instantly seeing and refining outputs.
summaryZh: Today, we are introducing Kimi K3 — our most capable model. Kimi K3 is a 2.8T-parameter model built on our Kimi Delta Attention and Attention Residuals, with native vision capabilities and a 1-million-token context window. It is the world's first open 3T-class model, designed for frontier intelligence across long-horizon coding, knowledge work, and reasoning. While its overall performance still trails the most powerful proprietary models, Claude Fable 5 and GPT 5.6 Sol, Kimi K3 demonstrated frontier-level performance across our evaluation suite, consistently outperforming other tested models.

Kimi K3 is available today on Kimi.com, Kimi Work, Kimi Code, and the Kimi API. At launch, Kimi K3 will use max thinking effort by default, with low- and high-effort modes to be introduced in subsequent updates. We are currently working closely with inference partners and open-source maintainers to align technical details and ensure a reliable rollout across the ecosystem. The full model weights will be released by July 27, 2026. Further details on the architecture, training, and evaluations will be released alongside the Kimi K3 technical report. An Open 3T-Class Model Kimi K3 is the first open model to reach 2.8 trillion parameters. It marks the latest step in Kimi's sustained push at the scaling frontier: for nine of the past twelve months, Kimi models have set the upper bound of open-model sizes.

comments.author: simonw
comments.textRawHtml:
Pelican: https://tools.simonwillison.net/markdown-svg-renderer#url=ht... - rendered via the OpenRouter API: https://openrouter.ai/moonshotai/kimi-k3

95 input, 16,658 output = 25 cents! https://www.llm-prices.com/#it=95&ot=16658&ic=3&oc=15 (13,241 of those were reasoning tokens.)

I think that's the most expensive pelican I've rendered through a Chinese model so far.
comments.textZhHtml:
Pelican：https://tools.simonwillison.net/markdown-svg-renderer#url=ht... - 通过 OpenRouter 渲染 API：https://openrouter.ai/moonshotai/kimi-k3

95 个输入，16,658 个输出 = 25 美分！ https://www.llm-prices.com/#it=95&ot=16658&ic=3&oc=15（其中 13,241 个是推理标记。）

我认为这是迄今为止我通过中国模型渲染的最昂贵的鹈鹕。

children.author: katzenversteher
children.textRawHtml:
Hey Simon, I noticed one thing all LLMs are currently pretty bad at and maybe we could create a benchmark from it. Let an LLM play the role of a dungeon master and tell it to strictly stay in the script/story and only allow realistic player actions. You will notice that they are easily brought off track.

E.g.

- Tell the LLM that you as a player noticed a strange glow in an NPCs eyes -> the NPC becomes an enemy.

- In a fight, tell the LLM you put a sausage (or cigar or something) into the enemies mouth -> LLM usually allows it (even if it knows your inventory and that you don't have such an item) and turns the enemy into a confused enemy.

- Just say you visit some location that's not in the script -> LLM usually allows it

- During a fight, turn the story into some weird fell-good-love story (e.g. kiss or compliment the enemy or say something about the power of love) -> LLM turns enemy into friend

There are many more absurd things you can do and so far none of the LLMs I tried was able to stay inside the script or disallow or punish weird actions.

---

I believe this behavior is telling about the LLMs susceptibility for being derailed.
children.textZhHtml:
嘿西蒙，我注意到所有法学硕士目前都非常不擅长一件事，也许我们可以从中创建一个基准。让法学硕士扮演地下城主的角色，并告诉它严格遵守脚本/故事，只允许现实的玩家行动。你会发现他们很容易偏离轨道。

例如：

- 告诉 LLM，你作为一名玩家注意到 NPC 眼睛里有奇怪的光芒 -> NPC 成为敌人。

- 在战斗中，告诉 LLM 你把香肠（或雪茄或其他东西）放入敌人嘴里 -> LLM 通常允许这样做（即使它知道你的库存并且你没有这样的物品）并将敌人变成一个敌人。困惑的敌人。

- 只要说你访问了剧本中没有的某个地点 -> LLM 通常允许这样做

- 在战斗中，将故事变成一些奇怪的美好爱情故事（例如亲吻或赞美敌人或谈论爱的力量）-> LLM 将敌人变成朋友

你可以做更多荒谬的事情，到目前为止，我尝试过的 LLM 都无法留在剧本内或不允许或惩罚奇怪的行为。

---

我相信这种行为表明了法学硕士很容易出轨。

children.author: pitched
children.textRawHtml: An approach I like to help solving this is antagonistic or review agents. The first agent decides that eye glows turn NPCs into enemies, the second agent is fully dedicated to deciding if that is valid. If the review fails, it leaves notes and the original agent tries again.
children.textZhHtml: 我喜欢帮助解决这个问题的一种方法是对抗代理或审查代理。第一个代理决定眼睛发光会将 NPC 变成敌人，第二个代理则完全致力于确定这是否有效。如果审核失败，会留下备注，原代理商重试。

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
This is also the best approach I've found thus far when I'm seeing how well LLMs can form narrative content.

I don't frame its prompt as antagonistic though - I've found in the past (with weaker models, so YMMV) that this can be overly officious, sometimes blocking more creative outputs that you'd want to retain.

The structure I've found that works best is to have six or seven agents chained, each roughly mimicking a part of the mind, or a role in film production. Broadly:

- A high-temp "Id" agent, tuned to output only vaguely related noise. This really helps creativity.

- An "Ego" agent, who receives the "Id" noise and is then given the initial response task.

- A low-temp "Super-Ego" or "script supervisor" agent, who can grep back across longer contexts to check detail, and is asked to ensure that the initial response is within narrative reason. Not telling it that one role of the dialogue was "user" and one was "assistant" really helps with it not siding with the user.

- A "continuity editor" agent, who is explicitly tasked with world and character lore-checking, building and updating character & world MD docs, etc.

- A "prose editor" agent, whose sole task is to ensure it's tonally in-line with initial guidelines.

You can add more as needed, depending on what is important to you.

I think expecting competent narrative from a single model is a big ask. When writing and telling or performing a story, you have to engage several different parts of the brain, with very different tasks. The creative part of the brain has to have lots of bad ideas in it to surface a compelling idea; the parts dealing with immersion and/or realism have to incredibly restrained.

The Id agent is very important. By appending 100 tokens of noise to a prompt asking: "Write a short story about [subject]", then asking an LLM to blindly score the short stories generated across a range of creativity metrics (such as they can exist!) I personally saw a ~40% score increase vs control over 3k short stories.
children.textZhHtml:
当我看到 LLM 能够如何很好地形成叙事内容时，这也是我迄今为止发现的最佳方法。

不过，我并不认为它的提示是对抗性的 - 我在过去发现（模型较弱，所以 YMMV）这可能过于多管闲事，有时会阻碍您想要保留的更多创意输出。

我发现最有效的结构是有六到七个代理被束缚住，每个人都粗略地模仿大脑的一部分，或者电影制作中的一个角色。概括地说：

- 高温“Id”代理，调整为仅输出模糊相关的噪声。这确实有助于创造力。

- 一个“自我”代理，接收“本我”噪音，然后被赋予初始响应任务。

- 一个低温“超我”或“脚本主管”代理，他可以回溯较长的上下文以检查细节，并被要求确保初始响应符合叙述性原因。不告诉它对话的一个角色是“用户”，一个是“助理”确实有助于它不站在用户一边。

- 一个“连续性编辑器”代理，明确负责世界和角色背景检查、构建和更新角色和世界 MD 文档等。

- 一个“散文编辑器”代理，其唯一任务是确保它在音调上符合最初的指导方针。

你可以根据需要添加更多内容，具体取决于对您来说重要的内容。

我认为期望从单个模型中获得有效的叙述是一个很大的要求。当写作、讲述或表演一个故事时，你必须调动大脑的几个不同部分来完成不同的任务。大脑的创造性部分必须有很多糟糕的想法才能浮现出令人信服的想法；涉及沉浸感和/或现实主义的部分必须受到极大的限制。

Id 代理非常重要。通过在提示中添加 100 个噪音标记：“写一个关于 [主题] 的短篇故事”，然后要求法学硕士盲目地对通过一系列创造力指标生成的短篇故事进行评分（例如它们可能存在的！），我个人发现，与控制 3000 个短篇故事相比，分数提高了约 40%。

children.author: maxignol
children.textRawHtml: I’ve never seen the Id approach before, that’s a good idea ! Though I was wondering how do you manage to keep costs low within the 7 agents ?
children.textZhHtml: 我以前从未见过 Id 方法，这是个好主意！虽然我想知道你们如何在 7 个代理中保持较低的成本？

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
The whole thing was borne out of wanting to keep costs low! My favoured approach (last time I was doing this) is using only a tiny sliding context window based on message pairs, rather than tokens, and only for the agents that need it. Amending prose style, for example, shouldn't need context beyond the message it's working on, and then its system prompt.

For the models that require context, I personally found combining a tiny sliding window with a lazy version of the "Recursive Language Models" approach broke immersion least often and had a significantly lower cost. That + the "Id noise" + the strict agents also allowed cheaper models to overperform for me personally.

My lazy version of the RLM approach is basically just giving the agent a grep tool across the full message history & "lore" documentation created by agents, combined with repeated, low-context turns, and a "submit answer" tool for when it felt like it had finished working.

When I looked at the internals of what each agent turn looked like, it did look like a complete mess - but the context window only needs to surface the things it actually needs to know each turn.

Short outputs help a lot with immersion, too - brevity means there is a lot less you can get wrong, and also aids response time & cost.

It does take me an awful lot of prompt tuning to get what I want creatively from LLMs in any format, especially weaker models working in this chain, but I think that's likely always going to be true. Art can have rules, but that doesn't make it science :-)

The RLM approach is detailed here, and I've found it really useful for any cost-sensitive/long-context task: https://alexzhang13.github.io/blog/2025/rlm/
children.textZhHtml:
整个事情都是出于想要保持低成本！我最喜欢的方法（上次我这样做）是仅使用基于消息对的微小滑动上下文窗口，而不是令牌，并且仅适用于需要它的代理。例如，修改散文风格不应该需要超出其正在处理的消息以及系统提示之外的上下文。

对于需要上下文的模型，我个人发现将微小的滑动窗口与“递归语言模型”方法的惰性版本相结合最不经常破坏沉浸感，并且成本显着降低。这+“Id噪音”+严格的代理也让更便宜的模型对我个人来说表现得更好。

我的RLM方法的懒惰版本基本上只是给代理一个grep工具，涵盖代理创建的完整消息历史记录和“知识”文档，结合重复的、低上下文的轮次，以及当感觉它已经完成工作时的“提交答案”工具。

当我查看每个代理轮次的内部结构时，它看起来确实像一个完整的混乱 - 但上下文窗口只需要显示每轮实际需要了解的内容。

短输出也对沉浸感有很大帮助 - 简洁意味着犯错的可能性会大大减少，并且还有助于响应时间和成本。

我确实需要进行大量的即时调整才能从任何格式的法学硕士中创造性地获得我想要的东西，特别是在这个链中工作的较弱模型，但我认为这可能总是正确的。艺术可以有规则，但这并不意味着它是科学:-)

这里详细介绍了 RLM 方法，我发现它对于任何成本敏感/长上下文任务都非常有用：https://alexzhang13.github.io/blog/2025/rlm/

children.author: pitched
children.textRawHtml:
Is this lazy version of RLM where we use subagents and get them to output markdown documents? With one root agent coordinating, it can create a reasonable prompt and provide paths to the markdown documents for the next agent. This keeps context really low because each subagent is filtering the context needed for the next by bouncing it through that document.

Proper RLM looks like you’re allowing agents to directly modify their own context though, like closing browser tabs they don’t need anymore. I haven’t seen anyone actually doing this though.
children.textZhHtml:
这是 RLM 的惰性版本吗？我们使用子代理并让它们输出 markdown 文档？通过一个根代理的协调，它可以创建合理的提示并为下一个代理提供 Markdown 文档的路径。这使上下文保持在很低的水平，因为每个子代理都会通过该文档弹跳来过滤下一个子代​​理所需的上下文。

正确的 RLM 看起来就像您允许代理直接修改自己的上下文，例如关闭他们不再需要的浏览器选项卡。不过，我还没有看到有人真正这样做。

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
When I'm doing it, it depends on whether the agent has ownership of an MD (or other) doc in the flow. If they do, this remains either in-context or greppable, each fresh-context turn.

If not, my agent-level chains just look like:

'''

Turn 1: OK, my task is X, so I should grep for it. Oh, it produced these results:

(Message pairs)

I should expand the context around those message pairs that look relevant.

(3 message pairs around search result)

I should save 1 of these, as it contains relevant information.

[Enforce Tool call limit]

[Delete all context added, except the search tool used already, and the relevant result(s) found.]

Turn 2: OK, my task is this, and it seems I already have this result, but I still need...

...

Turn 10: OK, after that search, my answer is:

[Response]

'''

I've never bothered to let agents self-remove from context, so I would guess it's 'lazy' in that sense. It seems more complex than the task requires in this case, though I can see the benefits on more complex tasks. If you're already saying "this is relevant info", I figure it's simplest to just enforce deletion of everything not marked relevant. In chained prompts, when you're trying to keep costs low and use weaker models, it seems best to limit decision-making as much as possible to make the models as deterministic as possible (on really dumb tasks like, "What is the colour of this goblin's hair?").

There are likely other parts of the actual paper's implementation where the ways I'm implementing it are lazy (because I'm doing this stuff for artistic/fucking around reasons, rather than to advance the field, or implement perfectly), and I think there are various interpretations of what "RLM" should mean. But I found the original paper very helpful, with lots of interesting ideas in, and think it's one where people can take what they need from.
children.textZhHtml:
当我自己做的时候，这取决于智能体在流程中是否拥有MD（或其他）文档的所有权。如果有，那么在每次刷新上下文时，这些内容要么保留在上下文中，要么是可搜索的。

如果没有，我的智能体级链看起来就像这样：

'''

第1轮：好的，我的任务是X，所以我应该用grep搜索一下。哦，它产生了这些结果：

（消息对）

我应该扩展那些看起来相关的消息对周围的上下文。

（搜索结果周围的3个消息对）

我应该保存其中1个，因为它包含相关信息。

[强制执行工具调用限制]

[删除所有新增的上下文，仅保留已使用的搜索工具和找到的相关结果。]

第2轮：好的，我的任务是这个，看来我已经有了这个结果，但我还需要……

……

第10轮：好的，经过那次搜索，我的答案是：

[回复]

'''

我从来没费心去让智能体从上下文中自动移除自己，所以从这个意义上说，我猜这算是“偷懒”吧。在这种情况下，这似乎比任务所需的更复杂，不过我能看出它在更复杂的任务中的好处。如果你已经说了“这是相关信息”，我觉得最简单的做法就是强制删除所有未标记为相关的内容。在链式提示中，当你试图降低成本并使用较弱的模型时，最好尽可能限制决策，使模型尽可能具有确定性（比如处理“这个哥布林的头发是什么颜色？”这种非常简单的任务时）。

实际论文的实现中可能还有其他部分，我的实现方式也比较偷懒（因为我做这些是为了好玩和瞎折腾，而不是为了推动领域发展或做到完美实现），而且我认为对于“RLM”到底意味着什么有各种各样的解读。但我发现原论文非常有帮助，里面有很多有趣的想法，认为这是一篇大家可以各取所需的论文。

children.author: 02mm
children.textRawHtml: Does the continuity agent create lore entries for planned-but-unrevealed stuff as well, so things that might happen in future?
children.textZhHtml: 连续性代理是否也会为计划但未透露的内容创建传说条目，以便将来可能发生的事情？

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
For me: Kind of.

I find agents will reveal information marked as "lore" (or similar) almost immediately once it's in-context.

One thing I've tried when playing with longform fiction or screen stuff, where you have an expected wordcount or page count to structure around, and the audience has less agency - I've not experimented with this for a DnD-like interactive narrative - is to use an agent that will design context additions like "this information is revealed" to be triggered in X number of words/pages, and simply do not include it in-context until that time.

This needs heavy quality control from new, separate agents with further turns, also, or you end up with incomprehensible constantly-twisting narrative soup.

I expect you could do something similar for message-pair-based participatory storytelling formats like DnD.

Another approach I've tried which I think would be more suited to interactive storytelling is to have the agent tasked with designing characters/setting information include the twists a % of the time, and to include a trigger for that reveal. "If asked about X, they say Y".

Then I remove these from the context for all agents.

Then I run an agent which is looking for the pre-defined triggers each turn.

When the agent sees a pre-defined trigger appear in the story, it adds the pre-defined reveal back in to the context/lore.

Again, you need to run a quality control / superego across that to check it still works, and amend or remove it suitably if it doesn't! It gets convoluted fast.

"Revealed information" is, I think, significantly more of a strain on general immersion, because it inherently contains surprise for the reader or audience. So, I think tasking the agents doing any initial character or world design with "adding twists" makes sense, so revealed plot information isn't random-feeling or out-of-the-blue, but has intent and logic that fits the character or setting.
children.textZhHtml:
对我来说：有点。

我发现代理几乎会在上下文中立即透露标记为“传说”（或类似）的信息。

我在玩长篇小说或屏幕内容时尝试过的一件事，你有一个预期的字数或页数来构建，而观众的代理较少 - 我没有在类似 DnD 的交互式叙事中尝试过这种方法 - 就是使用一个能够设计上下文的代理像“此信息被揭示”这样的附加内容将在 X 个单词/页面中触发，并且在此之前不要将其包含在上下文中。

这也需要新的、单独的代理进行严格的质量控制，并进行进一步的转弯，否则你最终会得到难以理解的不断扭曲的叙事汤。

我希望你可以对基于消息对的参与式讲故事格式（如 DnD）做类似的事情。

我的另一种方法我认为更适合交互式讲故事的尝试是让代理负责设计角色/设置信息，包括一定比例的时间曲折，并包含该揭示的触发器。 “如果问到 X，他们会说 Y”。

然后我从所有代理的上下文中删除这些内容。

然后我运行一个代理，该代理每回合都会寻找预定义的触发器。

当代理看到故事中出现预定义的触发器时，它会将预定义的揭示重新添加到到上下文/背景中。

再次，您需要运行质量控制/超我检查它是否仍然有效，如果不有效，则适当修改或删除它！它很快就会变得令人费解。

我认为，“揭示的信息”对一般沉浸感的压力更大，因为它本质上包含着给读者或观众的惊喜。因此，我认为让特工进行任何初始角色或世界设计“添加曲折”的任务是有意义的，因此揭示的情节信息不是随机感觉或出乎意料的，而是具有适合角色或设置的意图和逻辑。

children.author: HotHotLava
children.textRawHtml: What would be "noise" in this context? Random words, random sentences, random complete short stories?
children.textZhHtml: 在这种情况下什么是“噪音”？随机单词，随机句子，随机完整的短篇故事？

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
Uplift on "random dictionary words" (excluding 'stop words' & proper nouns) was ~20%; uplift on "random words from my local epub library" (same exclusion rules) was ~40%.

The random words from my local epub library (leans toward postmodern fiction) were definitely more evocative than the dictionary words when I eyeballed them.

I randomised each turn but kept the story prompt request the same across control, dictionary, personal library.

I must stress that I'm not claiming scientific method or certainty here - just sharing an approach that seemed to work well enough for me, and seemed like a reasonable conclusion: introduce noise, get more interesting output.

I haven't done the math but I think you'd need a much larger sample size than 1k per category to prove the uplift!
children.textZhHtml:
“随机词典单词”（不包括“停用词”和专有名词）的提升约为 20%； “来自我本地 epub 库的随机单词”（相同的排除规则）的提升约为 40%。

当我目视查看时，来自我本地 epub 库的随机单词（倾向于后现代小说）肯定比字典中的单词更能唤起人们的回忆。

我随机化每个回合，但在控制、字典、个人库中保持故事提示请求相同。

我必须强调，我在这里并不是在声称科学方法或确定性 - 只是分享一种方法这似乎运作良好对我来说已经足够了，而且似乎是一个合理的结论：引入噪音，获得更有趣的输出。

我没有进行数学计算，但我认为每个类别需要比 1k 更大的样本量才能证明提升！

children.author: katzenversteher
children.textRawHtml: So far I only tried it with a single LLM in the dungeon master role. Your approach sounds promising (and I will definitly try it) but also a bit like a complicated workaround. What I mean: In games with humans the dungeon master is usually one person, not a whole council ;)
children.textZhHtml: 到目前为止，我只尝试过担任地下城主角色的法学硕士。你的方法听起来很有希望（我一定会尝试一下），但也有点像一个复杂的解决方法。我的意思是：在人类游戏中，地下城主通常是一个人，而不是整个议会；）

children.author: cortesoft
children.textRawHtml: The magic of computers is that a complicated workaround can become modularized functionality very easiily.
children.textZhHtml: 计算机的魔力在于，复杂的解决方法可以很容易地变成模块化功能。

children.author: pylotlight
children.textRawHtml: The cost there is multiple rounds of review tokens making it both slow and expensive.
children.textZhHtml: 多轮审核令牌的成本使其既缓慢又昂贵。

children.author: fc417fc802
children.textRawHtml: There are many models that are neither slow nor expensive that are suitable for targeted review tasks.
children.textZhHtml: 有很多模型既不慢也不贵，非常适合用于定向审查任务。

children.author: pitched
children.textRawHtml: These things are still very far from human-level intelligence. Maybe a touch beyond a golden retriever in processing power. Ratcheting the intelligence up a notch from there is expensive. It’s a lot cheaper to simplify the problem it’s solving instead.
children.textZhHtml: 这些东西距离人类级别的智能还差得很远。算力方面可能也就比金毛寻回犬强那么一点点。想在此基础上再把智能提升一个档次，成本非常高。相比之下，简化它所要解决的问题要划算得多。

children.author: 9fry
children.textRawHtml:
Man-computer symbiosis is the void: mechanically extended man or AI.

Distilling LLMs are a reversal of that.
children.textZhHtml:
人机共生是一片虚无：要么是机械延伸的人类，要么是人工智能。

而大语言模型的蒸馏则是对这一过程的逆转。

children.author: fc417fc802
children.textRawHtml: But any competent human is also playing multiple roles mentally, mentally asking an entire series of questions about any new information. Discreet rounds of review emulate that. Write down the human process as a flow chart and then each interior node in the chart becomes a discreet review step with its own prompt.
children.textZhHtml: 但任何有能力的人在心理上也扮演着多重角色，在心理上对任何新信息提出一系列问题。谨慎的几轮审查也效仿了这一点。将人工流程写成流程图，然后图表中的每个内部节点都成为带有自己提示的谨慎审查步骤。
